description: This figure contains three subsections for two low-density medium Mn steels: M1 (Fe-8Mn-3Al-0.2C, no Si addition) and M2 (Fe-8Mn-3Al-1Si-0.2C, with 1 wt% Si addition). Subplots a₁ and a₂ display calculated room-temperature retained austenite fractions (via Koistinen-Marburger model) and Mₛ temperatures as functions of intercritical annealing (IA) temperature for M1 and M2, respectively: below the critical IA temperature (where Mₛ < room temperature), full retention of equilibrium austenite is expected, while retained austenite fraction decreases with increasing IA temperature above this threshold. Subplot b shows grain size distribution of both steels, indicating M1 has a higher fraction of fine grains (<0.5 μm) than M2, which improves austenite stability. Subplot c is a schematic illustrating elemental partitioning during IA: Mn and C diffuse from α ferrite to austenite in both steels, while Si additionally partitions from austenite to δ and α ferrite in M2. This figure supports that elemental segregation and fine grain effect lead to higher experimental retained austenite fractions than model predictions, and Si addition reduces austenite stability in M2.
